text:
University College Hospital 

National Hospital for Neurology and Neurosurgery 

Clinic Date: 02 October 2025 

Dr A. Patel  
Riverside Medical Practice  
48 Market Street, London SE15 3JT  

Dear Dr A. Patel,  

Daniel Moore, DOB: 22-09-1989, Hospital No: M842517 NHS No. 3049821765  
Flat 12, 5 Foundry Court, London, SE1 6QH 

Summary/ Diagnoses: History of seizures following head injury at work/persistent problems with concentration and fatigue/low mood features under review/smoker/vitamin D insufficiency. 

Medication: Levetiracetam 1000mg twice a day.  
Lamotrigine 50mg twice a day.  
Cholecalciferol 800 IU daily.  
Sertraline 50mg daily.  
Omeprazole 20mg daily.  

I reviewed him in clinic today. He is employed as a warehouse picker, working between tall racking and narrow aisles. He reports that lifting demands and pace vary by shift, with early mornings being the busiest. He describes improved sleep regularity since moving to fixed shifts, and has been using a phone reminder to space fluids and meals on longer picking runs. He remains independent in activities of daily living. On examination, gait was steady, no focal neurological deficit, and no nystagmus. Cardiovascular and respiratory examinations were unremarkable. 

Seizures: He brought a workplace incident log and a personal diary. Over the last six months he has recorded brief dissociative episodes at work on three occasions (March, May, and August), each lasting under two minutes, with preserved awareness and no convulsive features; no tongue biting or incontinence reported, and recovery was rapid without post-ictal confusion. He notes these events have tended to occur during high-paced picking in narrow aisles with heightened stress and noise. He has not had any events during sleep, and there have been no injuries or emergency attendances. Based on current history and pattern, the recent episodes are considered non-epileptic in nature, with no documented epileptic events in this interval. 

Occupational context: He avoids ladder work and uses a picker with a guard; he reports good compliance with site safety protocols. He is comfortable notifying supervisors to pause tasks briefly if aura-like sensations occur (described as a transient "head rush" with tight chest that settles with breathing techniques). No driving. 

Plan: 
- No change to anti-seizure medication today.  
- Continue seizure/symptom diary with clear differentiation of dissociative episodes versus any events with loss of awareness or motor features; provide event witness form to supervisors.  
- Refer to psychology for brief intervention targeting stress-related triggers and dissociative events.  
- Bloods in primary care: U&E, LFT, FBC, vitamin D re-check, and levetiracetam level if last test >12 months ago.  
- Occupational health note provided to support scheduled micro-breaks on high-intensity shifts and avoidance of lone working at height.  
- Safety: advise colleagues to place him in the recovery position if unwell; no restraint; call 999 if any prolonged loss of awareness or injury.  
- Review in 6 months, sooner if there is any event with loss of awareness, convulsion, or injury.  

Yours sincerely

entities.text: Over the last six months... the recent episodes are considered non-epileptic in nature, with no documented epileptic events in this interval.

check__Seizure Frequency Number.analysis: The letter’s Seizures section specifies that over the last six months there were only brief dissociative (non-epileptic) episodes and explicitly states there were no documented epileptic events in that interval. Therefore, epileptic seizure count over the past six months is zero, meeting the criterion for seizure-free for 6 months.
seizure_frequency_number: Over the last six months... the recent episodes are considered non-epileptic in nature, with no documented epileptic events in this interval.

flaws.description: Gold (seizure free for 6 month) disagrees with Reference (seizure free for multiple month)
